summary: The test suite covers all four core tag-generation rules (latest, pr-N, semver, branch-sha) and includes sanitization cases (lowercase, slash replacement, special chars, leading hyphens) plus integration/combined scenarios and deduplication. Design is clean: test classes map to distinct behaviours, names are descriptive, and fixtures are simple inline GitContext objects. Gaps in rigor hold it back: trailing hyphen stripping is never asserted; consecutive-hyphen collapsing is untested; there is no test verifying that main/master does NOT produce a branch-sha tag (only positive 'latest' presence is checked); short SHAs (<7 chars) and empty branch/SHA inputs are unhandled; pr_number=0 (falsy but not None) is not probed; and no test validates that entirely non-semver tag lists produce zero semver entries in the output. The TDD-labelled docstrings add narrative value but the actual test isolation is already good. Overall a solid, trustworthy suite for the happy paths with moderate edge-case depth.
